title: Update read/write paths to item_products
description: Repoint every product read/write path at the new global/item_products split: list_products_for_matching (runner.py's only entry point), create_product/update_product, the /catalogue-review and item-edit product CRUD routes and templates, and product-suggestion approval (must reuse the global find-or-create path, not create a second global product when two items independently suggest the same manufacturer/model).
acceptance_criteria: list_products_for_matching queries item_products JOIN products WHERE item_products.item_id = ? instead of products WHERE item_id = ?; catalogue.match() itself requires no changes
create_product/update_product operate against the global lookup-or-create path (FEATURE-1000) plus an item_products row (FEATURE-1001), replacing the current single-table item-scoped insert/update
Product-suggestion approval resolves against the global identity key before creating a product, attaching an item_products row to an existing global product where one already exists

Web UI product CRUD (create/edit/archive/delete, wanted toggle) operates on the item's item_products row for local fields and the shared products row for market fields, with no template exposing an item-scoped 'edit' control for a field that is now global without making clear it affects every project tracking that product
merge_products, find_duplicate_products, find_suspect_products, dedupe_products are updated for the global scope per FEATURE-1003 and remain usable as maintenance tools
notes: See docs/adr/0007-catalogue-globalization.md, Context section ('what already exists and doesn't need reinvention') for exactly which functions change vs. don't.
